heading: Procedure
body:
The following tutorial uses [mongoimport reference page](database-tools/mongoimport) to
load data from a `JSON` file to an Atlas cluster:

To run mongoimport to write to Atlas cluster, you
must specify a database user that has [readWrite](manual/reference/built-in-roles) privileges in the database
into which to import data.
For example, a user with [Atlas admin](manual/reference/built-in-roles)
role provides these privileges.

If no such user exists, create the user:

- If it isn't already displayed, click the
  Database Users tab.

- Click  Add New Database User.

- Add an Atlas admin user.

Click Connect for the Atlas cluster into which
you want to migrate data.

If the host where you will run mongoimport is not in the
IP Access List, update the list.
You can specify either:

- The public IP address of the server on which mongoimport
  will run, or

- If set up for VPC (Virtual Private Cloud) peering, either the peer's VPC (Virtual Private Cloud) CIDR (Classless Inter-Domain Routing) block
  (or a subnet) or the peer VPC (Virtual Private Cloud)'s Security Group, if you chose
  AWS (Amazon Web Services) as your cloud provider.

You can connect to your Atlas cluster using its
[connection string URI](manual/reference/connection-string). In
the connect dialog box perform the following steps:

- Click Drivers.

- Copy the connection string found in step 1.

- Replace **PASSWORD** with the password for the root user, and
  **DATABASE** with the name of the database to which you wish to connect.

  > **Important:** You must escape any instances of the `@` character in the
  > provided `<PASSWORD>`. For example, `p@ssword` should be
  > `p%40ssword`.

This connection string is specified to [mongoimport reference page](database-tools/mongoimport) in the
`--uri` option.

When using `--host`, if the Atlas cluster is a replica
set you must also retrieve the replica set name. For example:

```none
myAtlasRS/atlas-host1:27017,atlas-host2:27017,atlas-host3:27017
```
